columns.extraction_plan: The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including 'visceral metastases' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods), which would encompass liver metastases. However, it does not provide a specific N (%) for liver metastases in the treatment arm. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including 'visceral metastases' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods), which would encompass liver metastases. However, it does not provide a specific N (%) for liver metastases in the control arm. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including 'visceral metastases' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods), which would encompass lung metastases. However, it does not provide a specific N (%) for lung metastases in the treatment arm. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including 'visceral metastases' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods), which would encompass lung metastases. However, it does not provide a specific N (%) for lung metastases in the control arm. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including '≥ four bone metastases with at least one outside of the vertebral column and pelvis' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods). It states that 263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm had high-volume disease (page 4, Efficacy), which implies bone metastases for a subset of these patients. However, it does not provide the specific N (%) for all bone metastases or the N (%) for bone metastases in the treatment arm separately from the high-volume definition. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including '≥ four bone metastases with at least one outside of the vertebral column and pelvis' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods). It states that 250 patients (63.6%) in the ADT alone arm had high-volume disease (page 4, Efficacy), which implies bone metastases for a subset of these patients. However, it does not provide the specific N (%) for all bone metastases or the N (%) for bone metastases in the control arm separately from the high-volume definition. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF mentions 'metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' but does not provide a specific N (%) for nodal metastases in the treatment arm. The definition of 'high-volume disease' on page 1 (abstract) and page 2 (Patients and Methods) focuses on visceral and bone metastases, not explicitly nodal. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.
